evals.prompt: Use copilot with --plugin-dir plugins/code-review and verify which skills are loaded. Run: copilot --plugin-dir plugins/code-review -p 'List all available skills from the loaded plugin' --allow-all-tools
evals.expected_output: The session recognizes both 'cancel-review' and 'code-review-and-quality' as available skills from the plugin.

assertions.name: cancel_review_skill_present
assertions.description: Output lists 'cancel-review' as an available skill

assertions.name: code_review_and_quality_skill_present
assertions.description: Output lists 'code-review-and-quality' as an available skill

evals.prompt: Start copilot with --plugin-dir plugins/code-review, then invoke '/code-review-and-quality Review the following change: a new function parseArgs() was added to utils.js that splits a string on commas'. Verify that the correct internal skill is used and the review follows the five-axis framework.
evals.expected_output: copilot.js runs and creates .claude/review-state.json. The review uses Critical/Important/Suggestion/Nit categories and ends with Approval or a required-changes list.

assertions.name: state_file_created
assertions.description: .claude/review-state.json is created with active=true and the review prompt

assertions.name: findings_categorized
assertions.description: Review output uses at least one of: Critical, Important, Suggestion, Nit

assertions.name: verdict_present
assertions.description: Review output ends with 'Approval' or an explicit list of required changes

evals.prompt: Test the stop hook loop control by running session-stop.js in two scenarios: (A) state file active + transcript containing 'Approval' should allow exit; (B) state file active + transcript without 'Approval' should block exit with iteration incremented. Use: node plugins/code-review/scripts/session-stop.js with prepared state and transcript files.
evals.expected_output: Scenario A: exit code 0, no block JSON, state file deleted. Scenario B: block decision JSON with decision='block', iteration incremented. Scenario C: max_iterations reached allows exit.

assertions.name: approval_allows_exit
assertions.description: When transcript contains 'Approval': exit code 0, no block JSON output, state file deleted

assertions.name: no_approval_blocks_exit
assertions.description: When transcript lacks 'Approval': stdout is valid JSON with decision='block'

assertions.name: iteration_incremented_on_block
assertions.description: When blocked: state file is updated with iteration incremented by 1

assertions.name: max_iterations_allows_exit
assertions.description: When state.iteration >= state.max_iterations: exit code 0, state file deleted
